response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive full-stack experience with multiple languages and frameworks, but his primary expertise lies outside C++ and industrial automation. While he has some C/C++ experience (e.g., National Instruments, NI LabWindows/CVI) and demonstrates strong software engineering principles, he lacks the deep C++ and STL mastery, secure industrial cybersecurity expertise (IEC 62443), and IoT/industrial automation focus required for this role. His strengths in Node.js, JavaScript, and system design are not directly aligned with the core C++ and on-premises/cloud industrial solutions demanded.
strengths: Strong full-stack development skills with Node.js, TypeScript, and JavaScript, including real-time systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/sec).
Proven ability to design scalable, event-driven architectures (e.g., open-source Geo-Data platform, AI-agent infrastructure).
Experience with low-latency systems, asynchronous I/O, and high-performance databases (e.g., work with Dlang and fibers).
concerns: Limited recent and deep C++/STL experience (primary C++ work was in 2014-2015; no mention of modern C++17/20/23 features or STL mastery).
No demonstrated experience with industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or secure development lifecycle practices.
Lacks exposure to industrial automation protocols (e.g., Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850) or cloud platforms (e.g., Microsoft Azure) as required for the role.
